Items.Title: 如何使用 Rails 7 生成像 Dev.to（免费）这样的 OG 链接预览 | How to generate OG links preview like Dev.to (for free) with Rails 7
Items.Description:
嘿嘿嘿嘿嘿嘿
关于如何拥有美妆链接预览的快速文章，就像 Dev.to 一样

你会得到这样的东西：

是的，与“ like dev.to ”字面意思一样😛
我为我的新项目workby.io构建了类似的东西，这就是我构建它的方式......

我发现的几乎所有指南都在使用htmlcsstoimage.com ，事实上，dev.to 使用它。 它非常受欢迎且高效，但是，我不想为每 1k 图像支付 14 美元😂🐁

所以，我将使用Grover ：一个 Ruby gem，使用 Google Puppeteer 和 Chromium 将 HTML 转换为 PDF、PNG 或 JPEG。

如果你想用 Node.js 来构建它，你也可以关注我。 长话短说：我们将构建一个 HTML 视图并对其进行截图。

你可以创建一个空白的 Rails 项目，或者只是为你当前的应用程序添加额外的功能。

设置

安装 Grover gem
bundle add grover

以及 Grover、 Puppeteer的依赖项：
yarn add puppeteer

然后创建一个空控制器，您可以在其中使用不同的链接预览模板进行响应：
rails g controller og-imager dev_to

在dev_to动作中，让我们给 Grover 打电话，让他​​施展魔法
app/controllers/og_imager_controller.rb

 class OgImagerController < ApplicationController # GET /og_imager/dev_to # @param {string} title # @param {string} avatar # @param {string} username # @param {string} timestamp # @param {array} logos # @returns image/png def dev_to # Get params and set to variable # TODO: Retrieve your object instead :p @title = params [ :title ] @avatar = params [ :avatar ] @username = params [ :username ] @timestamp = params [ :timestamp ] @logos = params [ :logos ] # Grover.new accepts a URL or inline HTML and optional parameters for Puppeteer grover = Grover . new ( render_to_string ) # Get a screenshot png = grover . to_png # Render image send_data ( png , type: 'image/png' , disposition: 'inline' ) end end

这是我们的视图，只是简单的 HTML/CSS
app/views/og_imager/dev_to.html.erb

 <div class= "container" > <div class= "card" > <h1 class= "title" > < %= @ title % > </h1> <div class= "details" > <div class= "user" > <img src= "<%= @avatar %>" class= "avatar" > <p class= "username" > < %= @ username % > - < %= @ timestamp % > </p> </div> <div class= "logos" > < % @ logos.each do | logo | % > <img src= "<%= logo %>" class= "logo" > < % end % > </div> </div> </div> </div> <style> @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@400;500&display=swap') ; * { font-family : 'Roboto' , sans-serif ; color : #cd685f ; } .container { width : 1128px ; height : 600px ; display : flex ; } .card { display : flex ; justify-content : space-between ; flex-direction : column ; margin : 2.8rem 4rem 4rem 2.8rem ; border : 3px solid #c56b61 ; border-radius : 25px 25px 0px 0px ; box-shadow : 7px 10px 0px 1px #c56b61 ; padding : 3.8rem 1.8rem 1.8rem 1.8rem ; } .title { margin : 0px ; font-weight : 500 ; font-size : 5rem ; line-height : 80px ; } .details { display : flex ; justify-content : space-between ; } .user { display : flex ; align-items : center ; } .avatar { border : 3px solid #c56b61 ; width : 50px ; height : 50px ; border-radius : 50% ; margin-right : 10px ; } .username { margin : 0 ; font-size : 2.5rem ; } .logos { display : flex ; justify-content : space-between ; } .logo { width : 60px ; height : 60px ; margin-left : 20px ; transform : rotate ( 5deg ); } </style>

最后，创建一个初始化器来设置我们的浏览器视口（最终图像大小）。

initializers/grover.rb

 Grover . configure do | config | config . options = { viewport: { width: 1128 , height: 600 }, } end

如果您创建了一个空白项目，并且想将其用作微服务，这里有一个关于如何将这个东西部署到 Heroku 的指南。

  1. 在您的 Heroku 帐户上创建应用程序（假设您已经安装了 cli）： heroku create your_app_name
  2. 警告 Heroku 我们的 JS 库（Puppeteer）： heroku buildpacks:add heroku/nodejs --index=1
  3. 并帮助他设置 Puppeteer 依赖项😛： heroku buildpacks:add jontewks/puppeteer --index=2
  4. 告诉 Grover 在“无沙盒”中运行 Puppeteer： heroku config:set GROVER_NO_SANDBOX=true

要在链接预览中使用此图像，您将需要Open Graph 协议。 只是浏览器会寻找的简单元标记。

元标记始终打开

所以，
app/views/layouts/application.html.erb

然后要求 ERB 用

< %= content_for :head do % > <meta property= "og:title" content= "<%= @article.title %>" > <meta property= "og:image" content= "<%= article_link_preview(@article) %>" > <meta property= "og:image:type" content= "image/png" /> <meta property= "og:image:width" content= "1128" > <meta property= "og:image:height" content= "600" > <meta name= "twitter:card" content= "summary_large_image" /> < % end % >

在您的详细信息视图中。 喜欢： /articles/:id

上一篇文章article_link_preview方法是一个简单的帮助器，用于生成我们要查找的 URL。

app/helpers/articles_helper.rb

 def article_link_preview article uri = URI . parse ( 'https://your_app_name.herokuapp.com/ogimage' ) uri . query = URI . encode_www_form ( title: article . title , 'images[]' : article . tags . collect ( & :image ), timestamp: article . created_at . to_formatted_s ( :short ), ... ) uri . to_s end

你会得到这样的东西：
https://your_app_name.herokuapp.com/og_imager/dev_to?title=Real%20Time%20Notification%20System%20with%20Hotwire,%20in%20Rails%207&avatar=https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/user/profile_image/369241/a0111bcb-a046-4398-98cd-f4cf0e2f8d0d.png&username=Matias%20Carpintini&timestamp=13%20April&logos[]=https://img.icons8.com/office/80/000000/ruby-gemstone.png&logos[]=https://practicaldev-herokuapp-com.freetls.fastly.net/assets/devlogo-pwa-512.png

这是整个事情的回购。



原文:

Hey hey heyyyyyy
Quick article on how to have beauty link previews, just like Dev.to does

You're gonna get something like this:

Yup, was being literal with the "like dev.to" 😛
I've build something similar for my new project workby.io, here's how i've build it...

Almost every guide that i found was using htmlcsstoimage.com, in fact, dev.to uses it. It's pretty popular, and efficient, BUT, i don't want to pay $14 bucks for each 1k images 😂🐁

So, i'm going to use Grover: A Ruby gem to transform HTML into PDFs, PNGs or JPEGs using Google Puppeteer and Chromium.

If you want to build this with, let's say, Node.js, you can also follow me. Puppeteer is popular and easy to use.

Long story short: We're going to build a HTML view and take an screenshot of it.

You can create a blank Rails project, or just add additional functionality to your current app.

Setup

Install Grover gem with
bundle add grover

And the dependency of Grover, Puppeteer, with:
yarn add puppeteer

Then create an empty controller where you can respond with different link preview templates:
rails g controller og-imager dev_to

In the dev_to action, let's call Grover and let him do the magic
app/controllers/og_imager_controller.rb

class OgImagerController < ApplicationController # GET /og_imager/dev_to # @param {string} title # @param {string} avatar # @param {string} username # @param {string} timestamp # @param {array} logos # @returns image/png def dev_to # Get params and set to variable # TODO: Retrieve your object instead :p @title = params[:title] @avatar = params[:avatar] @username = params[:username] @timestamp = params[:timestamp] @logos = params[:logos] # Grover.new accepts a URL or inline HTML and optional parameters for Puppeteer grover = Grover.new( render_to_string ) # Get a screenshot png = grover.to_png # Render image send_data(png, type: 'image/png', disposition: 'inline') end
end

Here's our view, just simple HTML/CSS
app/views/og_imager/dev_to.html.erb

<div class="container"> <div class="card"> <h1 class="title"> <%= @title %> </h1> <div class="details"> <div class="user"> <img src="<%= @avatar %>" class="avatar"> <p class="username"> <%= @username %> - <%= @timestamp %> </p> </div> <div class="logos"> <% @logos.each do |logo| %> <img src="<%= logo %>" class="logo"> <% end %> </div> </div> </div>
</div> <style> @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@400;500&display=swap'); *{ font-family: 'Roboto', sans-serif; color: #cd685f; } .container{ width: 1128px; height: 600px; display: flex; } .card{ display: flex; justify-content: space-between; flex-direction: column; margin: 2.8rem 4rem 4rem 2.8rem; border: 3px solid #c56b61; border-radius: 25px 25px 0px 0px; box-shadow: 7px 10px 0px 1px #c56b61; padding: 3.8rem 1.8rem 1.8rem 1.8rem; } .title{ margin: 0px; font-weight: 500; font-size: 5rem; line-height: 80px; } .details{ display: flex; justify-content: space-between; } .user{ display: flex; align-items: center; } .avatar{ border: 3px solid #c56b61; width: 50px; height: 50px; border-radius: 50%; margin-right: 10px; } .username{ margin: 0; font-size: 2.5rem; } .logos{ display: flex; justify-content: space-between; } .logo{ width: 60px; height: 60px; margin-left: 20px; transform: rotate(5deg); }
</style>

And finally, create an initializer to set our Browser viewport (final image size).

initializers/grover.rb

Grover.configure do |config| config.options = { viewport: { width: 1128, height: 600 }, }
end

If you have created a blank project, and want to use this as a micro-service, here's a guide on how to deploy this thing to Heroku.

  1. Create the app on your Heroku account (T'm assuming you already have the cli installed): heroku create your_app_name
  2. Warn Heroku of our JS library (Puppeteer): heroku buildpacks:add heroku/nodejs --index=1
  3. And also help him to setup Puppeteer dependencies 😛: heroku buildpacks:add jontewks/puppeteer --index=2
  4. Tell Grover to run Puppeteer in the "no-sandbox": heroku config:set GROVER_NO_SANDBOX=true

To make use of this image on the link preview, you will need The Open Graph protocol. Just simple meta tags that browsers will look for.

Meta tags are always on

so,
app/views/layouts/application.html.erb

And then ask ERB to fill previous block with

<%= content_for :head do %> <meta property="og:title" content="<%= @article.title %>"> <meta property="og:image" content="<%= article_link_preview(@article) %>"> <meta property="og:image:type" content="image/png" /> <meta property="og:image:width" content="1128"> <meta property="og:image:height" content="600"> <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
<% end %>

at your details view. Like: /articles/:id

The twitter:card thing it's because they are more fancy on link previews, you can read more about that here

The previous article_link_preview method it's a simple helper that generates the URL that we are looking for.

app/helpers/articles_helper.rb

def article_link_preview article uri = URI.parse('https://your_app_name.herokuapp.com/ogimage') uri.query = URI.encode_www_form( title: article.title, 'images[]': article.tags.collect(&:image), timestamp: article.created_at.to_formatted_s(:short), ... ) uri.to_s
end

You will get something like this:
https://your_app_name.herokuapp.com/og_imager/dev_to?title=Real%20Time%20Notification%20System%20with%20Hotwire,%20in%20Rails%207&avatar=https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/user/profile_image/369241/a0111bcb-a046-4398-98cd-f4cf0e2f8d0d.png&username=Matias%20Carpintini&timestamp=13%20April&logos[]=https://img.icons8.com/office/80/000000/ruby-gemstone.png&logos[]=https://practicaldev-herokuapp-com.freetls.fastly.net/assets/devlogo-pwa-512.png

Items.Title: 第 1.2 节 - SnowPro 核心认证学习指南 | Section 1.2 - SnowPro Core Certification Study Guide
Items.Description:
亚历克·达彻

发表于本系列旨在作为个人学习指南。 信息可能不全面或不准确。 我分享它以防其他人发现它有用。 如果任何信息不准确，请随时发表评论。

返回考试指南

安全功能摘要

  • 多因素身份验证 (MFA)
    • 集成雪花功能
    • 由 Duo Security 提供支持，由 Snowflake 管理
    • 注册不是自动的，用户必须自己注册
    • 可以通过带有 MINS_TO_BYPASS_MFA 或 DISABLE_MFA 的 ALTER_USER 命令禁用
    • 可以缓存 MFA 令牌以减少连接时的提示次数
  • 数据加密
    • 端到端加密
      • 保护静态数据或往返于 Snowflake 的传输数据
      • 由客户网络、暂存区和安全 VPC 或 Vnet 组成
      • 暂存区可以是内部的（Snowflake 提供）或外部（客户提供）
      • 内部登台自动加密
      • 数据在转换过程中被解密并在之后重新加密
    • 客户端加密
      • 用户在将数据加载到雪花之前对其进行加密
      • 遵循云存储服务提供的特定协议
      • 客户创建一个与 Snowflake 共享的秘密主密钥
      • 云存储服务提供的客户端使用主密钥生成随机加密密钥，并在上传前加密文件
      • 上传文件和密钥，稍后下载
      • 客户端解密密钥，然后使用密钥解密文件
      • 云服务或任何第三方在任何时候都不会看到未加密的数据
      • 主密钥可以存储为 Snowflake 中的阶段对象 - 需要以 Base64 编码的 256 位高级加密标准 (AES) 密钥
  • 允许基于用户 IP 地址的受限访问
  • 可以使用定义为 CIDR 块的 IP 地址范围创建允许和阻止列表
  • 当前 IP 地址必须在允许列表中且不得在阻止列表中
  • 安全管理员（或更高级别）可以创建多个策略，但一次只能将一个策略与帐户关联
  • 策略可以应用于特定用户
  • 访问控制
    • 概述
      • 访问控制决定谁可以访问特定的 Snowflake 对象
      • 结合了自主访问控制（每个对象都有一个所有者）和基于角色的访问控制（分配给角色的权限）
      • 安全对象 = 可以授予访问权限的实体（默认情况下拒绝访问，除非授权允许）
      • 角色 = 可以授予权限的实体，依次分配给用户或其他角色（角色层次结构）
      • 权限 = 定义的对象访问级别
      • 用户 = Snowflake 识别的身份，无论是人还是程序
    • 注意事项
      • 帐户管理员（ACCOUNTADMIN 系统角色）可以查看和操作帐户中的所有对象
      • 仅将 ACCOUNTADMIN 分配给有限数量的用户
      • 要求具有 ACCOUNTADMIN 的用户使用 MFA
      • 将 ACCOUNTADMIN 分配给至少两个用户，以避免必须等待密码恢复过程
      • 避免使用 ACCOUNTADMIN 创建对象，除非它们需要最高级别的安全性
      • 不要让 ACCOUNTADMIN 成为任何人的默认角色
      • 避免将 ACCOUNTADMIN 用于自动化脚本
    • 安全数据库对象需要容器数据库和模式的 USAGE 权限
    • 自定义角色必须分配给用户或授予其他角色
    • 使用角色层次结构使访问与业务功能保持一致
    • 使用托管访问模式来增加对象安全性（消除对象所有者做出授权决定的能力）
    • 使用未来的授权来定义一组初始权限，这些权限应用于架构中特定类型的新对象
    • 一个用户不能查看另一个用户的查询结果
    • 在源对象上授予的权限不会转移到克隆对象
  • 访问控制权限
  • 配置访问控制
  • 联合身份验证和单点登录 (SSO)
    • 联合身份验证启用 SSO
      • 登录和退出 Snowflake
      • 由于不活动而导致系统超时
    • 使用 SSO，用户可以通过外部身份提供商 (IdP) 进行身份验证，并且可以在其 IdP 会话期间安全地启动 Snowflake 会话，而无需登录到 Snowflake
    • 登录
      • 登出
        • 标准要求 IdP 和 Snowflake 显式注销（所有 IdP 都支持）
        • 全局启用所有会话的注销并且依赖于 IdP
        • 超时
          • 返回考试指南

    • Anuvindh Sankaravilasam - Anuvindh Sankaravilasam - Feb 17 2月17日



    原文:

    Alec Dutcher

    Posted on Apr 28

    This series is intended to be a personal study guide. Information may not be comprehensive or accurate. I am sharing it in case others find it useful. Please feel free to comment if any information is inaccurate.

    Return to Exam Guide

    1.2 Outline security principles

    Summary of Security Features

    • Multi-factor Authentication (MFA)
      • Integrated Snowflake feature
      • Powered by Duo Security, managed by Snowflake
      • Enrollment is not automatic, users must enroll themselves
      • Can be disabled through the ALTER_USER command with MINS_TO_BYPASS_MFA or DISABLE_MFA
      • MFA tokens can be cached to reduce the number of prompts when connecting
    • Data Encryption
      • End-to-end encryption
        • Secures data at rest or in transit to and from Snowflake
        • Consists of a customer network, a staging area, and a secure VPC or Vnet
        • Staging areas can be internal (Snowflake provided) or external (customer provided)
        • Internal staging is automatically encrypted
        • All data at rest in Snowflake is automatically encrypted
        • Data is decrypted during transformation and re-encrypted afterward
      • Client-side encryption
        • User encrypts data before loading it into Snowflake
        • Follows specific protocol provided by cloud storage service
        • Customer creates a secret master key which is shared with Snowflake
        • Client provided by cloud storage service generates a random encryption key with the master key and encrypts the file before uploading
        • The file and the key are uploaded and later downloaded
        • The client decrypts the key and then decrypts the file using the key
        • At no point does the cloud service or any third party see the unencrypted data
        • The master key can be stored as a stage object in Snowflake - requires 256-bit Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) key encoded in Base64
    • Network and Security Policies
      • Allow restricted access based on user IP address
      • Can create allowed and blocked lists using IP address ranges defined as CIDR blocks
      • Current IP address must be in the allowed list and must not be in the blocked list
      • A security administrator (or higher) can create multiple policies, but only one can be associated with an account at a time
      • Policies can be applied to specific users
    • Access Control
      • Overview
        • Access control determines who can access specific Snowflake objects
        • Combines Discretionary Access Control (each object has an owner) and Role-based Access Control (privileges assigned to roles)
        • Securable object = entity to which access can be granted (denied by default unless allowed by a grant)
        • Role = entity to which privileges can be granted, in turn assigned to users or other roles (role hierarchy)
        • Privilege = defined level of access to an object
        • User = identity recognized by Snowflake, whether a person or program
      • Considerations
        • ACCOUNTADMIN Role
          • Account administrators (ACCOUNTADMIN system role) can view and operate on all objects in an account
          • Only assign ACCOUNTADMIN to a limited number of users
          • Require users with ACCOUNTADMIN to use MFA
          • Assign ACCOUNTADMIN to at least two users to avoid having to wait for password recovery procedures
          • Avoid using ACCOUNTADMIN to create objects unless they require the highest level of security
          • Don't make ACCOUNTADMIN anyone's default role
          • Avoid using ACCOUNTADMIN for automated scripts
        • Securable database objects require USAGE privilege on both the container database and schema
        • Custom roles must be assigned to users or granted to other roles
        • Use role hierarchies to align access with business functions
        • Use managed access schemas for added object security (removes object owners' ability to make grant decisions)
        • Use future grants to define an initial set of privileges applied to new objects of a certain type within a schema
        • A user cannot view the results of another user's query
        • Privileges granted on source objects are not transferred to cloned objects
      • Access Control Privileges
      • Configuring Access Control
    • Federated Authentication and Single Sign-On (SSO)
      • Federated authentication enables SSO
        • Logging into and out of Snowflake
        • System timeout due to inactivity
      • With SSO, users authenticate through an external identity provider (IdP) and can securely initiate Snowflake sessions during their IdP session without logging into Snowflake
      • SSO workflows
        • Login
          • Snowflake-initiated: user goes to Snowflake, chooses IdP, if successful IdP sends SAML response to Snowflake to initiate session
          • IdP initiated: user goes to IdP site and inputs credentials, then clicks on Snowflake application in the IdP, then IdP sends SAML response to Snowflake
        • Logout
          • Standard requires explicit logout of both IdP and Snowflake (supported by all IdPs)
          • Global enables logout of all sessions and is IdP-dependent
          • Snowflake-initiated: global not supported, each session must be explicitly logged out separately
          • IdP initiated: depends on IdP, can be standard and/or global
        • Timeout
          • Snowflake: web interface is disabled, user must re-authenticate through IdP or cancel the session
          • IdP: IdP times out, but open Snowflake sessions remain active

    Return to Exam Guide

    Anuvindh Sankaravilasam - Feb 17

Items.Title: 本周讨论和评论 - v2 | Discussion and Comment of the Week - v2
Items.Description:
这份每周综述重点介绍了我们认为本周最深思熟虑和/或最有趣的讨论。 我们还将在每一期中突出一个特别酷的评论。 因此，我们希望鼓励人们参与讨论并奖励那些在社区中发起或参与对话的人。 毕竟，社区是通过人们在其中互动而成为可能的。

本周讨论

对于本周的讨论，我想把焦点放在@adrvnc的“ 公开讨论：你对编写干净的代码有什么建议？ ”因为它引发了人们之间一些真正深思熟虑和友好的辩论。

听到关于什么构成干净代码以及人们在尝试自己编写干净代码时遵循的不同路线或规则的不同意见很酷。

希望在这次讨论中对每个人都有一些有用的建议。 嘿，如果你对干净的代码有自己的看法可以分享，那么放弃你的想法永远不会太晚！

本周评论

本周的评论发给@kspeakman ，他为提示“早期职业开发人员对这项工作有什么误解？ ”提出了这一精彩而简洁的建议， @ben开始了：

我可以想象，在早期，人们很容易过度专注于技术位而忽视你正在解决的实际问题。 我很欣赏 Kasey 如何精心制作这个解释并从个人经验中发表讲话，公开分享这是过去让他们绊倒的事情。 总是很高兴听到富有同情心和理解力的建议，但又尖锐又实用。

你的选择是什么？

这个社区里有很多很棒的讨论和评论。 我敦促大家在下面的评论中分享您最喜欢的评论和/或对过去一周的讨论。 如果你愿意，请给作者一个@mention——它可能会让他们感觉良好。 💚 💚



原文:

This weekly roundup highlights what we believe to be the most thoughtful and/or interesting discussion of the week. We're also be highlighting one particularly cool comment in each installment. 🙌

The DEV Community is particularly special because of the kind and thoughtful discussions happening between community members. As such, we want to encourage folks to participate in discussions and reward those who are initiating or taking part in conversations across the community. After all, a community is made possible by the people interacting inside it.

Discussion of the Week

For this week's discussion, I'd like to put the spotlight on @adrvnc's "Open Discussion: What Tips Do You Have For Writing Clean Code?" as it spurred some really thoughtful and friendly debate between folks.

It's cool to hear differing opinions on what constitutes clean code and the different routes or rules folks follow when trying to craft clean code themselves.

Hopefully, there's a bit of helpful advice for everyone in this discussion. And hey, never too late to drop your thoughts if you have your own perspective on clean code to share!

Comment of the Week

Comment of the week goes to @kspeakman for this brilliant and concise bit of advice to the prompt "What misconceptions do early-career devs have about this work?" that @ben kicked off:

I can imagine that early days it'd be easy to get hyper-focused on the technical bits and lose sight of the actual problem you're solving. I appreciated how Kasey crafted this explanation and spoke from personal experience, openly sharing that this is something that has tripped them up in the past. Always nice to hear advice that is empathetic and understanding, yet pointed and practical.

What are your picks?

There's loads of great discussions and comments floating about in this community. I urge you all to share your favorite comment and/or discussion of the past week below in the comments. And if you're up for it, give the author an @mention — it'll probably make 'em feel good. 💚

Items.Title: 点火（凉亭）数学库 | Ignition (Gazebo) Math Library
Items.Description:
杰马洛秋

发表于今天尝试在最新的ROS（noetic）环境下编译我2014年写的一个Gazebo插件，不出所料，出现错误，编译失败：

 fatal error: gazebo/math/gzmath.hh: No such file or directory 7 | #include <gazebo/math/gzmath.hh> | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ compilation terminated.

我首先在我的 PC 上检查 Gazebo 模拟器的版本，它是 Gazebo 11：

然后我找到这个页面，它展示了每个新 Gazebo 版本所需的迁移。

凉亭与点火

有趣的是，我看到命名空间“gazebo”已被“ignition”取代。 但为什么？

我在其官方网站上看到了答案：

我们将新的开发工作命名为 Igntion，以便将其与之前的 Gazebo 区分开来，后者现在被称为 Gazebo Classic。 这些变化进展顺利，这在很大程度上要归功于我们全球用户社区和其他利益相关者的贡献和支持。

我们以此为契机，改回了广为人知的名称“Gazebo”。 展望未来，现代机器人软件系列以前称为 Ignition，现在更名为 Gazebo。

点火数学文件夹结构

以下是存储库/usr/include/ignition/math6/中的所有文件，它们应该为机器人运动学提供完整的数学支持。

改进源代码

在本文档的帮助下，我更新了我以前的源代码的一些行。 我终于成功地构建了插件并获得了 .o 文件。

 #include <ignition/math.hh>
... // Load the controller void WifibotControllerPlugin :: Load ( physics :: ModelPtr _parent , sdf :: ElementPtr _sdf ) { ... ignition :: math :: Pose3d pose ; ignition :: math :: Vector3d pos ; #if GAZEBO_MAJOR_VERSION >= 8 pose = this -> parent -> WorldPose (); #else pose = this -> parent -> GetWorldPose (). Ign (); #endif robotPose [ 0 ] = pose . Pos (). X (); robotPose [ 1 ] = pose . Pos (). Y (); tf :: Quaternion qt ( pose . Rot (). X (), pose . Rot (). Y (), pose . Rot (). Z (), pose . Rot (). W () ); ... } ... void WifibotControllerPlugin :: write_position_data () { ignition :: math :: Pose3d orig_pose ; #if GAZEBO_MAJOR_VERSION >= 8 orig_pose = this -> parent -> WorldPose (); #else orig_pose = this -> parent -> GetWorldPose (). Ign (); #endif this -> parent -> SetWorldPose ( ignition :: math :: Pose3d ( robotPose [ 0 ], robotPose [ 1 ], 0 , 0 , 0 , robotPose [ 2 ] ) ); }

加载插件

现在，插件工作正常，我可以在模拟器中控制我的机器人：



原文:

jemaloQiu

Posted on Apr 28

Today I tried to compile an Gazebo plugin I wrote in 2014 in latest ROS (noetic) environment, unsurprisingly, there are errors and the compilation failed:

fatal error: gazebo/math/gzmath.hh: No such file or directory 7 | #include <gazebo/math/gzmath.hh>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.

I check firstly the version of Gazebo simulator on my PC, it is Gazebo 11:

Then I find this page which presents the migrations required for each new Gazebo verison.

Gazebo vs Ignition

Interesting, I see that the namespace "gazebo" has been replaced by "ignition". But why?

I see answer in its official site:

We branded the new development effort Igntion in order to differentiate it from Gazebo that had come before, which is now referred to as Gazebo Classic. Those changes have gone very well, thanks in no small part to contributions and support from our worldwide community of users and other stakeholders.

In 2022 we encountered a trademark obstacle regarding the use of the name "Ignition". We used this as an opportunity to switch back the widely known name "Gazebo". Going forward, the modern robotics software collection formerly known as Ignition, is now branded Gazebo.

Roadmap of Gazebo project:

Ignition Math folder structure

Below are all the files in repository /usr/include/ignition/math6/ which should provide full math support for robot kinematics.

├── math6 │ └── ignition │ ├── math │ │ ├── Angle.hh │ │ ├── AxisAlignedBox.hh │ │ ├── Box.hh │ │ ├── Capsule.hh │ │ ├── Color.hh │ │ ├── config.hh │ │ ├── Cylinder.hh │ │ ├── detail │ │ │ ├── Box.hh │ │ │ ├── Capsule.hh │ │ │ ├── Cylinder.hh │ │ │ ├── Ellipsoid.hh │ │ │ ├── Export.hh │ │ │ ├── Sphere.hh │ │ │ └── WellOrderedVector.hh │ │ ├── DiffDriveOdometry.hh │ │ ├── Ellipsoid.hh │ │ ├── Export.hh │ │ ├── Filter.hh │ │ ├── Frustum.hh │ │ ├── GaussMarkovProcess.hh │ │ ├── graph │ │ │ ├── Edge.hh │ │ │ ├── GraphAlgorithms.hh │ │ │ ├── Graph.hh │ │ │ └── Vertex.hh │ │ ├── Helpers.hh │ │ ├── Inertial.hh │ │ ├── Kmeans.hh │ │ ├── Line2.hh │ │ ├── Line3.hh │ │ ├── MassMatrix3.hh │ │ ├── Material.hh │ │ ├── MaterialType.hh │ │ ├── Matrix3.hh │ │ ├── Matrix4.hh │ │ ├── MovingWindowFilter.hh │ │ ├── OrientedBox.hh │ │ ├── PID.hh │ │ ├── Plane.hh │ │ ├── Pose3.hh │ │ ├── Quaternion.hh │ │ ├── Rand.hh │ │ ├── RollingMean.hh │ │ ├── RotationSpline.hh │ │ ├── SemanticVersion.hh │ │ ├── SignalStats.hh │ │ ├── SpeedLimiter.hh │ │ ├── Sphere.hh │ │ ├── SphericalCoordinates.hh │ │ ├── Spline.hh │ │ ├── Stopwatch.hh │ │ ├── Temperature.hh │ │ ├── Triangle3.hh │ │ ├── Triangle.hh │ │ ├── Vector2.hh │ │ ├── Vector3.hh │ │ ├── Vector3Stats.hh │ │ └── Vector4.hh

│ └── math.hh

Improve the source code

With help of this documentaiton, I have updated some lines of my former source code. And I finally successfully built the plugin and got the .o file.

#include <ignition/math.hh>
 ... // Load the controller
void WifibotControllerPlugin::Load(physics::ModelPtr _parent, sdf::ElementPtr _sdf)
{ ... ignition::math::Pose3d pose; ignition::math::Vector3d pos; #if GAZEBO_MAJOR_VERSION >= 8
 pose = this->parent->WorldPose();
#else
 pose = this->parent->GetWorldPose().Ign();
#endif
 robotPose[0] = pose.Pos().X(); robotPose[1] = pose.Pos().Y(); tf::Quaternion qt( pose.Rot().X(), pose.Rot().Y(), pose.Rot().Z(), pose.Rot().W() ); ...
} ... void WifibotControllerPlugin::write_position_data()
{ ignition::math::Pose3d orig_pose; #if GAZEBO_MAJOR_VERSION >= 8
 orig_pose = this->parent->WorldPose();
#else
 orig_pose = this->parent->GetWorldPose().Ign();
#endif
 this->parent->SetWorldPose(ignition::math::Pose3d (robotPose[0], robotPose[1], 0, 0, 0, robotPose[2] ) ); }

load the plugin

Now, the plugin is working properly and I can control my robot in the simulator:

Items.Title: Svelte 快速提示：样式属性默认值 | Svelte quick tip: Style prop defaults
Items.Description:
因此，您可以在组件样式中使用CSS 自定义属性...

 <p> I have something important to say. </p> <style> p { border : 2px solid var ( --line-color ); /* Decorative styles */ padding : 1rem ; max-width : 60ch ; } </style>

但是如果你想让你的样式有一个默认值呢？ 自定义属性var语法采用第二个参数，如果该属性未定义，则该参数设置回退。 因此，如果您希望默认边框颜色为darkred ，您可以执行以下操作。

 p { border : 2px solid var ( --line-color , darkred ); }

但是，如果您想在多个地方使用--line-color并具有相同的回退，这可能会变得冗长。 如果要更新默认值，则必须在多个地方进行！

 p { border : 2px solid var ( --line-color , darkred ); text-decoration : underline wavy var ( --line-color , darkred ) 1px ; }

有两种方法可以重构它以使其不那么冗长。 首先，您可以为默认值引入另一个自定义属性：

 p { --line-color-default : darkred ; border : 2px solid var ( --line-color , var ( --line-color-default )); text-decoration : underline wavy var ( --line-color , var ( --line-color-default )) 1px ; }

这样就可以在一个地方更改默认值，但是每次引用--line-color时，您仍然必须向var提供第二个参数。

相反，我推荐的方法是引入另一个表示line-color 或 fallback 的自定义属性。

 p { --_line-color : var ( --line-color , darkred ); border : 2px solid var ( --_line-color ); text-decoration : underline wavy var ( --_line-color ) 1px ; }

所以现在你有两个变量：

  • 您可以在此Svelte REPL中看到这一点。

    如果您打算在多个地方使用主题变量，则只需要引入像--_line-color这样的变量。 否则，在您使用该属性的地方设置回退是非常好的，如第一个示例中所示。

    这里展示的技术并不是 Svelte 独有的，它可以应用于任何使用自定义属性的地方。 但是，Svelte 对它特别感兴趣，因为自定义属性是推荐的组件主题化方式。



原文:

Svelte has a built-in solution for component theming using style props. So, you can use CSS custom properties in your component styles...

<p> I have something important to say.
</p> <style> p { border: 2px solid var(--line-color); /* Decorative styles */ padding: 1rem; max-width: 60ch; } </style>

...and easily set them from outside the component using style props.

<TextBox --line-color="mediumspringgreen"></TextBox>

But what if you want your style to have a default value? The custom property var syntax takes a second argument that sets a fallback if the property is not defined. So, if you wanted the default border color to be darkred, you could do the following.

p { border: 2px solid var(--line-color, darkred);
}

However, this can get verbose if you want to use --line-color in multiple places, with the same fallback. If you want to update the default value, you have to do it multiple places!

p { border: 2px solid var(--line-color, darkred); text-decoration: underline wavy var(--line-color, darkred) 1px;
}

There’s two ways to refactor this to make it less verbose. First, you could introduce another custom property for the default value:

p { --line-color-default: darkred; border: 2px solid var(--line-color, var(--line-color-default)); text-decoration: underline wavy var(--line-color, var(--line-color-default)) 1px;
}

This makes it so there’s one place to change the default value, but you still have to provide the second argument to var every time you reference --line-color.

Instead, my recommended approach would be to introduce another custom property that represents either line-color or the fallback.

p { --_line-color: var(--line-color, darkred); border: 2px solid var(--_line-color); text-decoration: underline wavy var(--_line-color) 1px;
}

So now you have two variables:

  • --line-color is the user-supplied theme value
  • --_line-color is what we use in our styles, and will either be the user-supplied value (if defined) or the default color

You can see this in action in this Svelte REPL.

You only need to introduce a variable like --_line-color if you plan on using the theme variable multiple places. Otherwise, it’s perfectly fine to set the fallback where you use the property, as in the first example.

The technique on display here is not unique to Svelte, and can be applied anywhere you use custom properties. However, it's of particular interest with Svelte, since custom properties are the recommended way of theming a component.

Items.Title: [安全报告] Auto Refresh Plus 的可疑行为 | [Security Report] Suspicious Behavior of Auto Refresh Plus
Items.Description:
大竹琢磨

发表于大家好。
这是预先用日语发表的文章（链接）的英文翻译和修改。

最近在开发 Web 应用程序时，我注意到一个不熟悉的第三方 cookie 附加到我正在开发的页面上。
经过仔细检查，我发现 cookie 是由浏览器扩展Auto Refresh Plus发出的，但它的行为非常可疑，因此我决定写这篇文章，因为它有大量的用户（超过 40 万）。 总之，我认为我应该停止使用这个扩展。

什么是利害攸关的

要寻找的可疑点数不胜数，但特别是以下几点真的很糟糕。

毫无意义和可疑的沟通

每次用户访问某个站点时，此扩展程序似乎都与名为“3001.scriptcdn.net”的站点进行通信。 顾名思义，这是一个将 JavaScript 文件和其他文件放置在外部 CDN（内容交付网络）上并可以在需要时下载的站点。 令人惊讶的是，除了这个扩展之外，这个站点没有用于任何其他用途，因为在域 scriptcdn.net 上的 Google 搜索不会找到一个单一的技术文档。 此外，令人怀疑的是，正在传输和下载的脚本文件对于扩展的工作是否必不可少。 这是因为部分下载的脚本如下

文件的 URL（引用自）： https ://3001.scriptcdn.net/code/static/1


 _0x32b3['fbQbAp'] = function(_0x38a00c) { var _0x37db2d = _0x31b03b(_0x38a00c); var _0x4cf70a = []; for (var _0x3c7b92 = 0x3a4 + 0x18a * -0x1 + -0x21a, _0x18c768 = _0x37db2d['length']; _0x3c7b92 < _0x18c768; _0x3c7b92++) { _0x4cf70a += '%' + ('00' + _0x37db2d['charCodeAt'](_0x3c7b92)['toString'](0x18f3 + 0x12e0 + 0x11 * -0x293))['slice'](-(0x24d9 + -0x1bca * 0x1 + 0x14b * -0x7)); } return decodeURIComponent(_0x4cf70a); }
 case '2': var _0x3ea808 = document['createElem' + 'ent'](_0x42e59b[_0x4c9219(-0x389, -0x2f9, -0x2fa, -0x26e)]); continue; case '3': Element[_0x3f3a92(-0x3ae, -0x2d5, -0x195, -0x407)][_0x4c9219(-0x527, -0x539, -0x469, -0x68c) + 'd'] = _0x481455['contentWin' + _0x4c9219(-0x334, -0x486, -0x44b, -0x34e)][_0x4c9219(-0x364, -0x36b, -0x254, -0x498)]['prototype'][_0x4c9219(-0x416, -0x539, -0x527, -0x3fc) + 'd']; continue; case '4': _0x481455[_0x4c9219(-0x478, -0x409, -0x337, -0x2e1)]['display'] = _0x42e59b['KUtJZ']; continue; case '5': _0x481455['src'] = _0x42e59b[_0x3f3a92(-0x493, -0x4bc, -0x5ed, -0x578)]; continue; case '6': document['body']['appendChil' + 'd'](_0x481455); continue;

乍一看，这个文件看起来像是一个写出要执行的程序的程序，而不是要执行的程序本身。 而且，“‘createElement’”的使用故意写成“‘createElem’+‘ent’”，似乎是为了避免被安全软件检测到。 此外，该文件还包含成人网站域列表。 这些不符合扩展的最初目的，显然是不自然的。 更重要的是，与本网站通信时发出的 cookie（如上所述）存储了两个可用于识别用户的令牌。 考虑到每次用户访问某个站点时都会进行通信这一事实，可以安全地假设这些令牌用于跟踪用户。 通常，扩展工作所需的脚本包含在安装文件中，因此每次用户从一个页面移动到另一个页面时都下载它们是不自然的。 即使扩展程序没有做任何可疑的事情，如果每次访问页面时都下载它也不是一件好事，因为它会减慢网站的加载速度（我使用的其他扩展程序不会以这种方式运行）。

顺便说一句，隐私政策说“我们不跟踪任何网站、网站使用情况等”。 写如下。

根本没有提供有关提供商的信息

有关提供此扩展的组织（或个人）的信息将被彻底隐瞒。
例如，chrome web store 中的开发者信息链接仅指向扩展的产品站点（ autorefresh.io ），唯一的支持联系人是产品站点上的联系表。 隐私政策中有一个用于个人信息查询的电子邮件地址，但由于该地址的域是autorefresh.io，因此尚不清楚究竟是谁处理此类查询。 如果您没有收到对您的电子邮件的回复，我们将无能为力，因为我们不知道公司名称（或公司名称）、电话号码或地址。

当然，使用 Cloudflare 可以很好地隐藏域名和 IP 地址。

他们不是自己做的

以下内容发布在此扩展程序的评论部分。

一位用户声称此扩展程序是“被盗”的。

我们已经调查了这篇文章的真实性，并确认确实在此扩展发布之前发布了具有相同名称和相同功能的扩展。
来源： https ://community.servicenow.com/community?id=community_question&sys_id=af0cafd9db3317c0200f0b55ca961940 但是，我们无法验证上面帖子中提到的“被盗”，因为扩展的原始版本已经关闭上市。 然而，提供现在公开可用的扩展的组织（或个人）确实没有开发扩展/出于某种原因重新发布了他们自己已经公开可用的扩展。 我们必须假设他们在某些特殊情况下会费心重新发布具有所有功能的免费产品，并且根本不会产生任何收入。

原版网址： https ://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/auto-refresh-plus/ohfjpkccecpdfkpmfocndhepolhljfhg


当前发布的扩展地址： https ://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/auto-refresh-plus-page-mo/hgeljhfekpckiplhkigfehkdpldcggm

应采取的应对措施

如果您正在使用此扩展程序，我们建议您立即将其卸载。 如果这不可能，请至少关闭“允许以隐身模式运行”。 另外，如果您是技术人员，请帮助我们验证扩展程序的行为。 验证行为后，如果您发现与本文所述相同的行为，请向商店报告。

笔记

  • 本文包含原创研究。 请自行承担使用本文中的信息的风险。
  • 本文内容仅为研究推测，并不暗示其绝对真实。 本文无意诽谤或诽谤任何第三方。 它也不打算造成任何不利。 感谢您阅读到最后。

迪普·K·萨西哈兰—— Apr 11 4月11日



原文:

Takuma Otake

Posted on Apr 28

Hello everybody.
This is an English translation and modification of the article (link) posted in Japanese in advance.

Recently while developing a web app, I noticed an unfamiliar third party cookie attached to a page I was developing.
Upon closer inspection, I discovered that the cookie was issued by the browser extension Auto Refresh Plus, but its behavior is so suspicious that I decided to write this article in light of the large number of users (over 400,000). In conclusion, I think I should stop using this extension.

*Please uninstall the program as it will continue to run as long as it exists in your browser.

What's at stake

There is no end to the number of suspicious points to look for, but the following points in particular are really bad.

Pointless and dubious communication

example.com is a site that does not normally grant cookies.

This extension seems to communicate with a site called "3001.scriptcdn.net" every time a user visits some site. The name suggests that this is a site where JavaScript files and other files are placed on an external CDN (Content Delivery Network) and can be downloaded when needed. Surprisingly, this site is not used for anything other than this extension, because a Google search on the domain scriptcdn.net does not turn up a single technical document. Also, it is doubtful that the script files being communicated and downloaded are essential for the extension to work. This is because some of the downloaded scripts are as follows

URL of the file (cited from): https://3001.scriptcdn.net/code/static/1


_0x32b3['fbQbAp'] = function(_0x38a00c) {
    var _0x37db2d = _0x31b03b(_0x38a00c);
    var _0x4cf70a = [];
    for (var _0x3c7b92 = 0x3a4 + 0x18a * -0x1 + -0x21a, _0x18c768 = _0x37db2d['length']; _0x3c7b92 < _0x18c768; _0x3c7b92++) {
        _0x4cf70a += '%' + ('00' + _0x37db2d['charCodeAt'](_0x3c7b92)['toString'](0x18f3 + 0x12e0 + 0x11 * -0x293))['slice'](-(0x24d9 + -0x1bca * 0x1 + 0x14b * -0x7));
    }
    return decodeURIComponent(_0x4cf70a);
}

case '2':
    var _0x3ea808 = document['createElem' + 'ent'](_0x42e59b[_0x4c9219(-0x389, -0x2f9, -0x2fa, -0x26e)]);
    continue;
case '3':
    Element[_0x3f3a92(-0x3ae, -0x2d5, -0x195, -0x407)][_0x4c9219(-0x527, -0x539, -0x469, -0x68c) + 'd'] = _0x481455['contentWin' + _0x4c9219(-0x334, -0x486, -0x44b, -0x34e)][_0x4c9219(-0x364, -0x36b, -0x254, -0x498)]['prototype'][_0x4c9219(-0x416, -0x539, -0x527, -0x3fc) + 'd'];
    continue;
case '4':
    _0x481455[_0x4c9219(-0x478, -0x409, -0x337, -0x2e1)]['display'] = _0x42e59b['KUtJZ'];
    continue;
case '5':
    _0x481455['src'] = _0x42e59b[_0x3f3a92(-0x493, -0x4bc, -0x5ed, -0x578)];
    continue;
case '6':
    document['body']['appendChil' + 'd'](_0x481455);
    continue;

At a glance, this file looks like a program for writing out a program to be executed, rather than a program itself to be executed. Moreover, the use of "'createElement'" is deliberately written as "'createElem' + 'ent'", which seems to be an attempt to avoid detection by security software. In addition, the file also contains a list of adult site domains. These are not in line with the original purpose of the extension and are clearly unnatural. More to the point, the cookie issued when communicating with this site (as mentioned above) stores two tokens that can be used to identify the user. Considering the fact that the communication is made every time the user accesses some site, it is safe to assume that these tokens are used to track the user. Generally, the scripts necessary for extensions to work are included in the installation file, so it would be unnatural for them to be downloaded every time the user moves from one page to another. Even if the extension is not doing anything at all suspicious, it is not a good thing if it downloads every time you visit a page, because it slows down the loading of the site (other extensions I use do not behave in this way).

By the way, the privacy policy says "We do not track any websites, website usage, etc." is written as follows.

No information on the provider is provided at all

Information about the organization (or individual) providing this extension is thoroughly withheld.
For example, the developer information link in the chrome web store only leads to the extension's product site (autorefresh.io), and the only support contact is a contact form on the product site. There is an email address in the privacy policy for personal information inquiries, but since the domain of that address is autorefresh.io, it is not clear who exactly handles such inquiries. If you do not receive a reply to your email, there is nothing we can do because we do not know the company name (or name of the company), phone number, or address.

Of course, both the domain name and IP address are well hidden using Cloudflare.

They didn't make it themselves

The following was posted in the review section of this extension.

One user claims that this extension is "stolen".

We have investigated the authenticity of this post and have confirmed that indeed, an extension with the same name and the same functionality was published before this extension was released.
Source: https://community.servicenow.com/community?id=community_question&sys_id=af0cafd9db3317c0200f0b55ca961940 However, we were not able to verify the "stolen" mentioned in the post above, as the original version of the extension has already been closed to the public. However, it is true that the organization (or individual) providing the extensions that are now publicly available did not develop the extensions/re-released the extensions that were already publicly available on their own for some reason. We have to assume that there was some special circumstance for them to go to the trouble of re-releasing something that is available with all the features for free and does not generate any revenue at all.

URL of the original version: https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/auto-refresh-plus/ohfjpkccecpdfkpmfocndhepolhljfhg


URL of the currently released extension: https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/auto-refresh-plus-page-mo/hgeljhfekpckiiplhkigfehkdpldcggm

Responses to be taken

If you are using this extension, we recommend that you uninstall it immediately. If this is not possible, please at least turn off "Allow to run in incognito mode". Also, if you are a technician, please help us verify the behavior of the extension. Once you have verified the behavior, please report to the store if you see the same behavior as described in this article.

*I have reported it to Chrome Web Store and Microsoft Edge add-on store.

Notes

  • This article contains original research. Please use the information in this article at your own risk.
  • The content of this article is only speculation based on research, and is not intended to imply that it is absolutely true. This article is not intended to defame or slander any third party. It is also not intended to cause any disadvantage. ## Lastly It has been a long article explaining many things. Thank you for reading to the end.

Items.Title: 使用 Sort 神奇地将 Moonbirds NFT 销售导入 Google 表格 | Magically import Moonbirds NFT sales into Google Sheets using Sort
Items.Description:
使用Sort API ，我们可以将任何以太坊交易数据输入 Google Sheets。 特别是对于Moonbirds ，我们可以为 Moonbirds 创建嵌套鸟类、销售、转让或在区块链上发生的任何事件的 Google 表格。 对于本教程，我们将专注于销售（跨 OpenSea、LooksRare 和 Rarible 交易所，Coinbase 即将推出）。

我们将专注于单个交易，为了简化教程，我们将使用一个名为SyncWith的 Google 表格插件，该插件可用于发送 API 调用并将结果存储在 Google 表格中。

安装 SyncWith Google 表格插件

  1. 打开一个新的Google Sheet，在菜单栏中，点击“Extensions” -> “Add-ons” -> “Get add-ons”，搜索“SyncWith”并安装。

  2. 安装插件后，通过“扩展”->“SyncWith”->“管理连接”访问插件

  3. 滚动到“SyncWith”应用程序的最底部，然后单击“自定义”

  4. 使用 JSON 正文创建 POST 请求

创建排序 API 请求

让我们为 Moonbirds 数据制定 API 请求

帖子正文：

 { "nft_event": "sale", "nft_address": "0x23581767a106ae21c074b2276D25e5C3e136a68b", "limit" : 50, "fields" : { "nft" : 1 } }
  • 限制：从 API 请求返回的结果数（最大值为 250，要进一步返回历史数据，请使用“时间戳”字段）
  • 字段：响应会很大，我们只返回“nft”字段（包括所有有用的 NFT 数据），而忽略其他数据，例如日志和函数参数。 要同时包含时间戳，请将此值更改为 { "timestamp" : 1, "nft" : 1 }

现在将 API 请求放入“SyncWith”中

点击预览

塑造数据结果

删除“id”、“status”和“object”字段（通过单击垃圾桶图标），单击“result”字段上的“expand”

接下来，“展开”“nft”字段，单击“更新”。

全部做完！ 您应该在 Google 表格中有一份最新的 Moonbirds 销售列表！

您可以继续进一步扩展“属性”列，指定不同的日期来获取历史数据（使用“时间戳”API 字段），或者进行更详细的分析。

我们也可以做其他事情，比如寻找执行了“toggleNesting”功能的区块链交易（在鸟巢/未巢时执行）：

 { "contract_address": "0x23581767a106ae21c074b2276d25e5c3e136a68b", "contract_function": "toggleNesting", "limit" : 50 }

希望这是使用Sort API将数据导入 Google 表格的一个很好的入门。 请访问我们的Discord提出任何问题，或继续关注使用 Moonbirds 交易数据的更多教程。



原文:

Using the Sort API, we can get any Ethereum transaction data into Google Sheets. Specifically for Moonbirds, we can create a Google Sheet of nesting birds, sales, transfers, or any event that occurred on the blockchain for Moonbirds. For this tutorial, we'll focus on sales (across OpenSea, LooksRare, and Rarible exchanges, Coinbase coming soon).

The Sort API allows us to look at individual blockchain transactions, or even perform millisecond aggregations (average, sum, etc, useful for charting). We'll focus on the individual transactions, and to simplify the tutorial, we'll use a Google Sheets plugin called SyncWith, useful for sending API calls and storing the result in a Google Sheet.

Install SyncWith Google Sheets Add-on

  1. Open a new Google Sheet, in the menu bar, click "Extensions" -> "Add-ons" -> "Get add-ons", search for "SyncWith" and install.

  2. After the plugin is installed, visit the plugin via "Extensions" -> "SyncWith" -> "Manage Connections"

  3. Scroll to the very bottom of the "SyncWith" app and click "Custom"

  4. Create a POST request with a JSON body

Create a Sort API Request

Let's formulate our API request for Moonbirds data

API endpoint:

https://api.sort.xyz/v0/transaction

POST body:

{
  "nft_event": "sale",
  "nft_address": "0x23581767a106ae21c074b2276D25e5C3e136a68b",
  "limit" : 50,
  "fields" : { "nft" : 1 }
}
  • nft_event: only look for sales
  • nft_address: contract address of NFT
  • limit: number of results to return from API request (max is 250, to go further back for historical data, use the "timestamp" field)
  • fields: the responses will be large, let's only return the "nft" field (which includes all useful NFT data), and leave out other data such as logs and function parameters. To also include a timestamp, change this value to { "timestamp" : 1, "nft" : 1 }

(if you're familiar with Dune Analytics, think of Sort as an API-first version of Dune)

Now put the API request in "SyncWith"

Click Preview

Shape the data result

There is a list of results in the "result" field.

Delete the "id", "status", and "object" fields (by clicking the trash can icon), click "expand" on the "result" field

Next, "expand" the "nft" field, click "Update".

All done! You should have a list of the latest Moonbirds sales in Google Sheets!

You can continue further to expand the "attributes" column, specify different dates to get historical data (using the "timestamp" API field), or perform more detailed analysis.

We can do other things too, such as looking for blockchain transactions that have executed the "toggleNesting" function (executed when a bird nests/un-nests):

{
  "contract_address": 
  "0x23581767a106ae21c074b2276d25e5c3e136a68b",
  "contract_function": "toggleNesting",
  "limit" : 50
}

Hopefully this has been a good primer on using the Sort API to get data into Google Sheets. Please visit us in Discord to ask any questions, or stay tuned for more tutorials using Moonbirds transaction data.

Items.Description:
我的灵感来自于我小时候在我父亲的手机里玩的流行的诺基亚蛇游戏。 我玩得很开心。 此外，我的朋友过去常常吹嘘他们的分数，但没有记分牌来跟踪他们。 这就是为什么我决定用高分板建造一个🤠。

为了让它更有趣和更具挑战性，我添加了一点点扭曲。 每当出现金色水果时，您将获得 50 分以上，但有 20% 的机会您的分数将减少 50 分😜。 那么你愿意接受挑战吗？ 与您的朋友竞争并在排行榜上名列前茅！

立即播放： Snake Arcade

提交类别：

原文:

Snake Arcade is a retro themed snake game with a live highscore board. I took this inspiration from the popular Nokia snake game which I used to play in my dad's mobile back when I was a kid. I had so much fun playing it. Moreover my friends used to brag about their scores but there's was no scoreboard to track them. And that's why I decided to build one with a highscore board 🤠.

To make it more fun and challenging I have added a little twist. Whenever the golden fruit appears, you get 50 points more but there's a 20% chance that your score will be reduced by 50 points 😜. So are you willing to take the challenge? Compete with your friends and top the leaderboard!

Play now: Snake Arcade
